title: Bidens rostrata
taxonomy: Bidens rostrata is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Bidens, which is a group of annual or perennial herbs. The species Bidens rostrata is native to North America and is often found in wetlands. It is closely related to other Bidens species, such as Bidens cernua and Bidens aristosa. The classification of Bidens rostrata is further refined at the infraspecific level, but the exact classification may vary depending on the source.
uses: Bidens rostrata has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help soothe skin irritations and wounds. In some cultures, Bidens rostrata is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its purported ability to regulate bowel movements. Additionally, the plant's leaves are sometimes used to treat fever and coughs. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses and ensure safety.

intro: Bidens rostrata, commonly known as Bidens or bur marigold, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Bidens rostrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Bidens rostrata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
